raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Two-thirds of California’s community college students struggle with basic needs insecurities such as food and housing.",
    "The Bay Area's astronomical cost of living poses a significant hurdle for local community college students.",
    "West Valley-Mission Community College District has successfully balanced its budget in recent years without imposing student fees.",
    "Over 5,000 students have enrolled in the College Promise Scholarship program at West Valley-Mission Community College District.",
    "The passage of AB 3158 will simplify the process of accessing free tuition for students.",
    "AB 3158 allows every student in the service area, regardless of their enrollment status, to access free, high-quality education.",
    "The law aims to remove financial or procedural obstacles that have held students back from pursuing education.",
    "Community colleges serve a diverse range of students who may not fit into the traditional college mold.",
    "The bill aims to ensure equity in education and make sure that no one is left behind.",
    "The implementation of AB 3158 does not increase the District’s budget or require more state funding.",
    "WVMCCD has seen a resurgence in overall enrollment following the COVID-19 Pandemic.",
    "The district is set to broaden its impact with new bachelor’s degrees and workforce development programs."
  ]
}
```
